sections.paragraph:
φορτίον, ου, τό (in form, a dim. of φόρτος; Hes.+; IG IV2/1, 123, 6 [IV b.c.], pap, LXX; TestJob 18:7; Joseph.; Tat. 23, 1)

① that which constitutes a load for transport, load lit. φορτίον βαστάζειν (Teles p. 10 H.; Herm. Wr. 10, 8b) Hs 9, 2, 4. Of the cargo of a ship (Hes., X. et al.; Jos., Ant. 14, 377; POxy 1153, 9 [I a.d.]) Ac 27:10 (v.l. φόρτος).

② that which is carried and constitutes a burden, burden, in imagery (Epict. 2, 9, 22; 4, 13, 16), of the oppressive burden of the law Mt 23:4; Lk 11:46ab. Cp. Mt 11:30. φορτίον βαστάζειν (Diog. L. 7, 170; Pythagorean in Stob., Flor. 85, 15 V 680 H.) Gal 6:5 (one is to be concerned about one’s own burden, rather than compare oneself complacently w. others [perh. gnomic fut. Burton, MT §69; Betz, Gal. 303f; cp. 2 Cor 5:10; DKuck, NTS 40, ’94, 289–97, apocalyptic motif.]).—Frisk s.v. φέρω B 10. M-M. TW.

dim. dim. = diminutive

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Teles Teles , III b.c.—List 5

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

Stob Stob , V a.d.—List 5

perh. perh. = perhaps

fut. fut. = fut.

Burton, MT Burton, MT = EB., Syntax of the Moods and Tenses in New Testament Greek—List 6

Betz, Gal. Betz, Gal. = Galatians (Hermeneia)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

NTS NTS = New Testament Studies—List 6

Frisk Frisk = HF., Griechisches etymologisches Wörterbuch—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1064
